---
title: "Compositional data analysis of the Gong & Sharma et al. dataset"
author: "Christian Halter"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(),'%e de %B, %Y')`"
output: 
  rmdformats::downcute:
    lightbox: true
    thumbnails: false
    self_contained: true
    gallery: true
    code_folding: show
  pkgdown:
    as_is: true
---


# Load libraries

```{r, include=FALSE, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
# renv::init()
# renv::snapshot()

library(pROC)
source("functions.R")

my_seed <- 123
set.seed(my_seed)
```

# Set paths

```{r}
ds_name <- "GongSharma_2024_PrePrintTBD"

path_nas <- file.path("", "Volumes", "Shared", "DataCollections", "Standardized_SingleCell_Datasets")
path_root <- file.path(path_nas, ds_name)
path_data <- file.path(path_root, "data")
path_plots <- file.path(getwd(), "plots")
```


# Create synthetic dataset

```{r}
#' Simulate cell composition for multiple samples.
#'
#' This function generates a matrix of cell counts for different cell types across
#' multiple samples, using either a Dirichlet-Multinomial or a simple
#' Proportions-Multinomial model. The number of cell types is determined
#' by the length of the provided proportion vector.
#'
#' @param n_samples The number of samples to simulate.
#' @param mean_cells The mean number of cells per sample.
#' @param min_cells_per_sample The minimum number of cells per sample.
#' @param max_cells_per_sample The maximum number of cells per sample.
#' @param method The method for simulating proportions: "dirichlet" (default)
#'   or "simple_sd".
#' @param dirichlet_alpha A numeric vector of alpha parameters for the
#'   Dirichlet distribution. Required if method is "dirichlet".
#' @param mean_proportions A numeric vector of mean proportions for each cell
#'   type. Required if method is "simple_sd". These will be automatically scaled to sum to 1.
#' @param prop_sd The relative standard deviation (coefficient of variation) for 
#'   adding noise to proportions. For example, 0.1 means 10% relative variation.
#'   Required if method is "simple_sd".
#'
#' @return A matrix with dimensions (n_samples, n_cts) containing simulated cell counts.
#'
#' @examples
#' # Example 1: Use Dirichlet distribution
#' set.seed(123)
#' sim_dirichlet <- simulate_cell_composition(
#'   n_samples = 5,
#'   mean_cells = 1000,
#'   method = "dirichlet",
#'   dirichlet_alpha = c(5, 2, 1)
#' )
#' print(sim_dirichlet)
#'
#' # Example 2: Use simple standard deviation with unnormalized proportions
#' set.seed(123)
#' sim_simple <- simulate_cell_composition(
#'   n_samples = 5,
#'   mean_cells = 1000,
#'   method = "simple_sd",
#'   mean_proportions = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1, 0.5), # Sums to 1.5, will be scaled
#'   prop_sd = 0.05
#' )
#' print(sim_simple)
simulate_cell_composition <- function(
  n_samples,
  mean_cells,
  min_cells_per_sample = NULL,
  max_cells_per_sample = NULL,
  method = "dirichlet",
  dirichlet_alpha = NULL,
  mean_proportions = NULL,
  prop_sd = NULL,
  base_sd = 5  # numeric, e.g. 5 means ±5 cells noise
) {
  # --- Step 1: validation (same as before) ---
  if (!(method %in% c("dirichlet", "simple_sd"))) {
    stop("Invalid method. Choose 'dirichlet' or 'simple_sd'.")
  }

  if (method == "dirichlet") {
    if (is.null(dirichlet_alpha)) stop("'dirichlet_alpha' must be provided for 'dirichlet' method.")
    if (!requireNamespace("gtools", quietly = TRUE)) {
      install.packages("gtools")
    }
    library(gtools)
    n_cts <- length(dirichlet_alpha)
  } else {
    if (is.null(mean_proportions) || is.null(prop_sd)) {
      stop("'mean_proportions' and 'prop_sd' must be provided for 'simple_sd'.")
    }
    n_cts <- length(mean_proportions)
    mean_proportions <- mean_proportions / sum(mean_proportions)
    cat("Note: The provided 'mean_proportions' have been normalized to sum to 1.\n")
  }

  if (is.null(min_cells_per_sample)) min_cells_per_sample <- floor(0.75 * mean_cells)
  if (is.null(max_cells_per_sample)) max_cells_per_sample <- floor(1.25 * mean_cells)

  # --- Step 2: total cells per sample ---
  total_cells_per_sample <- sample(
    seq(min_cells_per_sample, max_cells_per_sample),
    n_samples,
    replace = TRUE
  )

  # --- Step 3: proportions ---
  if (method == "dirichlet") {
    proportions_matrix <- gtools::rdirichlet(n_samples, dirichlet_alpha)
  } else {
    proportions_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_cts)
    for (i in 1:n_samples) {
      relative_sds <- mean_proportions * prop_sd
      noisy_proportions <- pmax(0, rnorm(n_cts, mean = mean_proportions, sd = relative_sds))
      proportions_matrix[i, ] <- noisy_proportions / sum(noisy_proportions)
    }
  }

  # --- Step 4: simulate counts ---
  cell_counts_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_cts)
  for (i in 1:n_samples) {
    counts <- as.vector(rmultinom(
      1,
      size = total_cells_per_sample[i],
      prob = proportions_matrix[i, ]
    ))

    # Add optional noise
    if (!is.null(base_sd) && base_sd > 0) {
      noise <- round(rnorm(n_cts, mean = 0, sd = base_sd))
      counts <- counts + noise
      counts[counts < 0] <- 0  # avoid negatives
    }

    cell_counts_matrix[i, ] <- counts
  }

  # --- Step 5: names ---
  colnames(cell_counts_matrix) <- paste0("Cell_Type_", 1:n_cts)
  rownames(cell_counts_matrix) <- paste0("Sample_", 1:n_samples)

  return(as.data.frame(cell_counts_matrix))
}
```



```{r}
# Define the start and end values for the sequence
start_val <- 0.1
end_val <- 50
num_samples <- 50

# 1. Generate the initial exponential sequence
# This sequence will have a total sum greater than 100
log_seq <- seq(log(start_val), log(end_val), length.out = num_samples)
exponential_sequence <- exp(log_seq)

# 2. Normalize the sequence to ensure the sum is 1
# This is done by dividing each element by the current sum
current_sum <- sum(exponential_sequence)
normalized_sequence <- exponential_sequence / current_sum

# 3. Scale the normalized sequence so its sum is 100
# We multiply each element by the target sum
constrained_sequence <- normalized_sequence * 100

mean_props <- constrained_sequence



n_samples <- 50
test <- simulate_cell_composition(
  n_samples = n_samples,
  mean_cells = 15000,
  min_cells_per_sample = 10000,
  max_cells_per_sample = 20000,
  mean_proportions = mean_props,
  method = "simple_sd",
  prop_sd = 0.4
)

plot_pca(test, labels = c(rep(1, n_samples/2), rep(2, n_samples/2)))
get_ct_var(test)
df_freq <- test %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df()
get_ct_var(df_freq)
ggsave(filename = file.path(path_plots, "semisynthetic_MeanVariance_percent_fit.pdf"), width = 3, height = 2.5)
plot((colMeans(df_freq)), (Rfast::colVars(df_freq)))
plot_data <- data.frame(
  mean = log(colMeans(df_freq)),
  variance = log(Rfast::colVars(df_freq))
)
# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = mean, y = variance)) +
  geom_point() +
  # geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "log( Mean (in %) )",
    y = "log( Variance (in %) )"
  ) +
  theme_classic2() # Optional: a clean theme
ggsave(filename = file.path(path_plots, "synthetic_MeanVariance_percentLogLog.pdf"), width = 3, height = 2.5)
get_ct_var(df_freq %>% clr())
ggsave(filename = file.path(path_plots, "synthetic_MeanVariance_CLR_fit.pdf"), width = 3, height = 2.5)

mean_props[1:round(length(mean_props) / 2)] <- mean_props[1:round(length(mean_props) / 2)] * 4

test1 <- simulate_cell_composition(
  n_samples = n_samples,
  mean_cells = 15000,
  min_cells_per_sample = 10000,
  max_cells_per_sample = 20000,
  mean_proportions = mean_props,
  method = "simple_sd",
  prop_sd = 0.4
)
rownames(test1) <- paste0(rownames(test1), "_new")

plot_pca(test1, labels = c(rep(1, n_samples/2), rep(2, n_samples/2)))


test_both <- rbind(test, test1)


get_ct_var(test_both)
df_freq <- test_both %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df()
get_ct_var(df_freq)
ggsave(filename = file.path(path_plots, "synthetic_MeanVariance_percent_fit_2groups.pdf"), width = 3, height = 2.5)
plot((colMeans(df_freq)), (Rfast::colVars(df_freq)))
plot_data <- data.frame(
  mean = log(colMeans(df_freq)),
  variance = log(Rfast::colVars(df_freq))
)
# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = mean, y = variance)) +
  geom_point() +
  # geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "log( Mean (in %) )",
    y = "log( Variance (in %) )"
  ) +
  theme_classic2() # Optional: a clean theme
ggsave(filename = file.path(path_plots, "synthetic_MeanVariance_percentLogLog_2groups.pdf"), width = 3, height = 2.5)
get_ct_var(df_freq %>% clr())
ggsave(filename = file.path(path_plots, "synthetic_MeanVariance_CLR_fit_2groups.pdf"), width = 3, height = 2.5)


plot_pca(test_both, labels = c(rep(1, n_samples), rep(2, n_samples)))
plot_pca(test_both %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df(), labels = c(rep(1, n_samples), rep(2, n_samples)))
plot_pca(test_both %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df() %>% clr(), labels = c(rep(1, n_samples), rep(2, n_samples)))
```


```{r}
n <- 10
mean_props <- seq(from = 1 / (n + 1), to = n / (n + 1), length.out = n)

test <- simulate_cell_composition(
  n_samples = 20,
  mean_cells = 10000,
  min_cells_per_sample = 5000,
  max_cells_per_sample = 20000,
  mean_proportions = mean_props,
  method = "simple_sd",
  prop_sd = 0.5
)

plot_pca(test, labels = c(rep(1, 10), rep(2, 10)))
get_ct_var(as.data.frame(test))
get_ct_var(as.data.frame(test) %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df() %>% clr())


test1 <- simulate_cell_composition(
  n_samples = 20,
  mean_cells = 10000,
  min_cells_per_sample = 5000,
  max_cells_per_sample = 20000,
  mean_proportions = rev(mean_props),
  method = "simple_sd",
  prop_sd = 0.5
)
rownames(test1) <- paste0(rownames(test1), "_new")

plot_pca(test1, labels = c(rep(1, 10), rep(2, 10)))


test_both <- rbind(test, test1)

plot_pca(test_both, labels = c(rep(1, 20), rep(2, 20)))
plot_pca(test_both %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df(), labels = c(rep(1, 20), rep(2, 20)))
plot_pca(test_both %>% impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>% calc_perc_df() %>% clr(), labels = c(rep(1, 20), rep(2, 20)))
```





# Define functions

```{r}
library(scales)

# Function to add the log2 scale to a plot
add_log_scale <- function(p, scale = "log2") {
  p + scale_x_continuous(trans = scale)
}

plot_results <- function(results, n_ct_to_select, log_perturbation = FALSE) {
  plot_list <- list()
  for (nct in n_ct_to_select) {
    res_all <- results[results$n_celltypes == nct, ]
    res_avg <- res_all %>%
      group_by(trans_method, Amount_of_perturbation) %>%
      mutate(Silhouette_score = mean(Silhouette_score),
             ANOSIM_score = mean(ANOSIM_score),
             Modularity_score = mean(Modularity_score),
             Adjusted_Rand_Index = mean(Adjusted_Rand_Index)) %>%
      ungroup() %>% 
      distinct(interaction(trans_method, Amount_of_perturbation), .keep_all=TRUE)
    
    p_list <- list()
    p_list[["plot_sil"]] <- ggplot() +
      geom_line(data = res_avg, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Silhouette_score, color = trans_method), size = 1.2) +
      ylim(c(min(res_avg$Silhouette_score), 1)) +
      theme_classic() +
      # geom_point(data = res_all, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Silhouette_score, color = trans_method)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Differential cell types: ", nct, "\nSilhouette score"))
    p_list[["plot_mod"]] <- ggplot() +
      geom_line(data = res_avg, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Modularity_score, color = trans_method), size = 1.2) +
      ylim(c(min(res_avg$Modularity_score), 1)) +
      theme_classic() +
      # geom_point(data = res_all, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Modularity_score, color = trans_method)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Differential cell types: ", nct, "\nModularity score"))
    p_list[["plot_clust"]] <- ggplot() +
      geom_line(data = res_avg, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Adjusted_Rand_Index, color = trans_method), size = 1.2) +
      ylim(c(min(res_avg$Adjusted_Rand_Index), 1)) +
      theme_classic() +
      # geom_point(data = res_all, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Modularity_score, color = trans_method)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Differential cell types: ", nct, "\nClustering score"))
    p_list[["plot_anosim"]] <- ggplot() +
      geom_line(data = res_avg, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = ANOSIM_score, color = trans_method), size = 1.2) +
      ylim(c(min(res_avg$ANOSIM_score), 1)) +
      theme_classic() +
      # geom_point(data = res_all, aes(x = Amount_of_perturbation, y = Silhouette_score, color = trans_method)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Differential cell types: ", nct, "\nANOSIM score"))
    
    if (log_perturbation) {
      p_list <- lapply(p_list, add_log_scale)
    }
    
    p <- ggarrange(plotlist = p_list, nrow=1, common.legend = TRUE)
    plot_list[[as.character(nct)]] <- p
  }
  
  return(plot_list)
}
```




# Plot arcsin-sqrt function

```{r}
# Install and load ggplot2 if you haven't already
# install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)

# Create a data frame for plotting the function
# The domain of the function is x from 0 to 1
plot_data <- data.frame(
  x = seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 100)
)

# Calculate the arcsine square root of x
# The R function is asin(sqrt(x))
plot_data$y <- asin(sqrt(plot_data$x))

# Create the plot with annotations
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  # Add the line for the function
  geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1.2) +
  # Set the title and axis labels using LaTeX syntax
  labs(
    title = expression("Plot of the Arcsine Square Root Function:" * italic(y) * " = " * arcsin(sqrt(italic(x)))),
    x = expression(italic(x)),
    y = expression(arcsin(sqrt(italic(x))))
  ) +
  # Set the plot limits
  xlim(c(0, 1)) +
  ylim(c(0, pi/2)) +
  # Add grid lines
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "gray"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "gray")
  ) +
  # Add annotations for the domain and range
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.1,
    y = 1.5,
    label = "x-range: [0, 1]",
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.1,
    y = 1.4,
    label = expression(paste("y-range: [0, ", pi/2, "]")),
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 4
  )
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "arcsin_sqrt_function_visualized.pdf"),
       width = 5,
       height = 3)
```
